selected_example: It would be pointless to engage in hypothesis before we have the facts.

definition: based on situations or ideas that are possible and imagined rather than real and true

selected_example: I wasn't asking about anybody in particular—it was a purely hypothetical question.

definition: a small symbol on a computer or smartphone screen that represents a program or a file | a famous person or thing that people admire and see as a symbol of a particular idea, way of life, etc.

selected_example: Click on the printer icon with the mouse. | Sandra has become a style icon, for the fashion world particularly.

definition: a painting or statue of a holy person that is also thought of as a holy object
original_example: Click on the printer icon with the mouse.
selected_example: Click on the printer icon with the mouse.

definition: [psychology] the part of the unconscious mind where many of a person’s basic needs, feelings and desires are supposed to exist

selected_example: You must carry ID at all times.

definition: an idea or standard that seems perfect, and worth trying to achieve or obtain | a person or thing that you think is perfect

selected_example: He was accused of betraying his political ideals. | It's my ideal of what a family home should be.

definition: similar in every detail | the same

definition: the process of showing, proving or recognizing who or what sb/sth is | the process of recognizing that sth exists, or is important | official papers or a document that can prove who you are | a strong feeling of sympathy, understanding or support for sb/sth | the process of making a close connection between one person or thing and another

definition: based on or connected with an ideology

selected_example: This was rejected for ideological reasons.

definition: a set of ideas that an economic or political system is based on | a set of beliefs, especially one held by a particular group, that influences the way people behave

synonym: set of beliefs | set of beliefs
selected_example: Marxist/capitalist ideology | the ideology of gender roles

definition: [informal] a rude way to refer to sb who you think is very stupid | [offensive] a word for a person with very low intelligence that is now considered offensive

definition: a lack of knowledge or information about sth | the state of not knowing about sth and so being unable to worry about it | if you do not know about sth, you cannot worry about it

selected_example: They fought a long battle against prejudice and ignorance. | We preferred to remain in blissful ignorance of what was going on. | Some doctors believe ignorance is bliss and don't give their patients all the facts.

selected_example: Never make your students feel ignorant. | At that time I was ignorant of events going on elsewhere. | You're behaving like a stupid ignorant child!

definition: a false idea or belief, especially about sb or about a situation | sth that seems to exist but in fact does not, or seems to be sth that it is not

definition: language that produces pictures in the minds of people reading or listening | [formal] pictures, photographs, etc.

selected_example: poetic imagery | satellite imagery (= for example, photographs of the earth taken from space)

definition: a situation in which two or more things are not the same size or are not treated the same, in a way that is unfair or causes problems

selected_example: Attempts are being made to redress (= put right) the imbalance between our import and export figures.

definition: not fully developed or grown

selected_example: The immature birds have shorter tails and brown, speckled plumage.

definition: behaving in a way that is not sensible and is typical of people who are much younger
original_example: immature behaviour
selected_example: immature behaviour

definition: extremely large or great

selected_example: There is still an immense amount of work to be done.

definition: the process of coming to live permanently in a different country from the one you were born in; the number of people who do this | the place at a port, an airport, etc. where the passports and other documents of people coming into a country are checked

selected_example: We are strongly supportive of immigration and multiculturalism. | We landed at Heathrow and went through customs and immigration.

definition: likely to happen very soon

selected_example: The system is in imminent danger of collapse.

definition: the state of living or lasting forever

selected_example: He is well on his way to show business immortality.

definition: that cannot catch or be affected by a particular disease or illness

selected_example: Adults are often immune to German measles.

definition: not affected by sth, especially sth that you might expect to be harmful | protected from sth and therefore able to avoid it

selected_example: You'll eventually become immune to criticism. | No one should be immune from prosecution.

definition: having a major impact or effect

selected_example: It was a hugely impactful advertising campaign.

definition: [formal] to damage sth or make sth worse

selected_example: His age impaired his chances of finding a new job.

definition: [formal] to make sth that has been officially decided start to happen or be used

definition: [formal] the act of making sth that has been officially decided start to happen or be used

selected_example: the implementation of the new system

definition: to show or suggest that sb is involved in sth bad or criminal | to show or suggest that sth is the cause of sth bad | to be involved in a crime; to be responsible for sth bad

selected_example: He tried to avoid saying anything that would implicate him further. | The results implicate poor hygiene as one cause of the outbreak. | Senior officials were implicated in the scandal.

definition: a possible effect or result of an action or a decision | the fact of being involved, or of involving sb, in sth, especially a crime

selected_example: They failed to consider the wider implications of their actions. | He resigned after his implication in a sex scandal.

definition: sth that is suggested or indirectly stated (= sth that is implied)

definition: suggested without being directly expressed

selected_example: Implicit in his speech was the assumption that they were guilty.

definition: forming part of sth (although perhaps not directly expressed) | complete and not doubted

selected_example: The ability to listen is implicit in the teacher's role. | She had the implicit trust of her staff.

definition: to suggest that sth is true or that you feel or think sth, without saying so directly | to make it seem likely that sth is true or exists

selected_example: Are you implying (that) I am wrong? | The survey implies (that) more people are moving house than was thought.

definition: to make sth necessary in order to be successful

selected_example: The project implies an enormous investment in training.

definition: to introduce a new law, rule, tax, etc.; to order that a rule, punishment, etc. be used | to force sb/sth to have to deal with sth that is difficult or unpleasant | to make sb accept the same opinions, wishes, etc. as your own | to expect sb to do sth for you or to spend time with you, when it may not be convenient for them | to make sb/sth accept or be aware of your presence or ideas

definition: the act of putting sb in a prison or another place from which they cannot escape; the state of being there

definition: a sudden strong wish or need to do sth, without stopping to think about the results | [specialist] a force or movement of energy that causes sth else to react | [formal] sth that causes sb/sth to do sth or to develop and make progress

definition: the fact of not being able to do sth

selected_example: Some families go without medical treatment because of their inability to pay.

definition: not enough; not good enough | not able, or not confident enough, to deal with a situation

definition: not suitable or appropriate in a particular situation

definition: [formal] the start of an institution, an organization, etc.

definition: [formal] the extent to which sth happens or has an effect | [physics] the way in which light meets a surface

definition: [formal] a slope

selected_example: a steep/slight incline

definition: wanting to do sth | tending to do sth; likely to do sth | used when you are expressing an opinion but do not want to express it very strongly | having a natural ability for sth; preferring to do sth | sloping; at an angle

definition: the lack of skill or ability to do your job or a task as it should be done

definition: trouble or problems, especially in connection with what you need or would like yourself | a person or thing that causes problems or difficulties

definition: to include sth so that it forms a part of sth | [business] to create a legally recognized company

definition: not accurate or true | speaking or behaving in a way that does not follow the accepted standards or rules

definition: a list of names or topics that are referred to in a book, etc., usually arranged at the end of a book in alphabetical order or listed in a separate file or book | a box of cards with information on them, arranged in alphabetical order | a number giving a measurement of sth compared with a particular standard | [computing] a list of the records in a computer file with information about where each is located
